title: cyperus cyperinus var. laxatus
intro: Cyperus cyperinus var. laxatus, also known as looseflower umbrella sedge, is a type of sedge plant belonging to the Cyperaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also valued for its health benefits, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support digestive health. The herbal preparations of Cyperus cyperinus var. laxatus typically involve infusing the roots and rhizomes in water or using them in decoctions to create teas, tinctures, or infusions for therapeutic purposes.
